name: Day-1 New Repo
desc: First contact with an unfamiliar codebase: what is it, is it broken, is it safe, can I work on it.
tagline: The four questions to ask any repo you just cloned.

name: Pre-Launch
desc: The night-before checks: safe, accessible, deployable, ready for a bad day.
tagline: Everything you should check before you press deploy.

name: Spring Cleaning
desc: Make it smaller and clearer: delete, simplify, then map what debt remains.
tagline: Cut the dead weight, then see what's really left.

name: Weekly Vitals
desc: Ten minutes, trend arrows. Re-run every week.
tagline: A ten-minute weekly health read with trend arrows.

name: Agent Day-1
desc: First contact with an agent codebase: read its mind, its hands, its loops, its traces.
tagline: Understand an AI-agent codebase before you touch it.

name: Agent Ship-Check
desc: Before an agent touches real users: guarded, evaluated, supervised, affordable.
tagline: Is your agent safe to put in front of real users?

name: Triage & Fix
desc: Close the loop: recon names the audits this repo needs, you run your picks, then the Fixer turns their findings into verified commits.
tagline: From “what's wrong?” to shipped fixes, one loop.

name: Retrieval Tune-Up
desc: For RAG-shaped agents: how context gets built, whether retrieval returns the right things, and the evals that keep it that way.
tagline: Make your RAG agent retrieve the right things.

name: Face-Lift
desc: The visual overhaul: hierarchy first, then layout, type, and color — and the Fixer turns the findings into commits.
tagline: A guided visual overhaul that ends in real commits.

name: Wayfinding
desc: Can a user find their way around? Map the navigation and menus, check what wins the eye on every screen, and confirm it all still works on a phone.
tagline: Can users find their way around — on desktop and on a phone?

name: Make Data Legible
desc: The visualization pass: make every chart tell the truth, every dashboard answer its question at a glance, the number that matters win the eye, and the palette stay readable for everyone.
tagline: Make every chart and dashboard tell the truth at a glance.

name: Total UI Overhaul
desc: The interaction-and-UI sweep: navigation, menus, every interactive state, the empty screens, and the icon language — then the Fixer turns the findings into verified commits.
tagline: Overhaul the whole UI layer — then ship the fixes as commits.

name: Founder Funnel
desc: One niche, end to end: map it, prove the pain, tear down the field, size it, position the entry, stress the model, then rule.
tagline: Pressure-test a startup idea before you write a line.

name: Gut Check
desc: Seventy-two hours of truth for one idea: is the pain real, who already fights for the money, go or no.
tagline: 72 hours of truth for one idea: go or no.

name: Ship to the World
desc: Reach every user: usable in any language, accessible to everyone, and findable by search and shared links.
tagline: Make it usable, accessible, and findable everywhere.

name: OSS Release-Ready
desc: Before the repo goes public: safe to expose, dependencies sound, and every license accounted for.
tagline: Everything to check before a repo goes public.

name: eCommerce Optimization
desc: Optimize the whole funnel for a first-time visitor: audit every conversion surface, then cut friction, sharpen the visual hierarchy, tighten the copy, and make it findable — and the Fixer ships the highest-leverage lifts as commits.
tagline: Audit every conversion surface for a newcomer, then ship the biggest lifts as commits.

name: Experience Optimization
desc: Get it, want it, keep it — walk every surface a visitor meets and optimize the whole experience: does a newcomer understand it, are the benefits shown not told, do the conversion surfaces and their proof earn the yes, does the first session land a win, and does anything bring them back — then the Fixer ships the biggest lifts as commits.
tagline: Comprehension to conversion to retention — audit every surface a visitor meets, then ship the biggest lifts as commits.

name: Ship-It Week
desc: A limited, themed sprint for launch season — hunt the bugs, lock the doors, make it fast, rehearse the bad day, and get found. One themed bundle, five reports, ready to ship.
tagline: Launch season, in one themed bundle of five audits.

name: Deep Code Review
desc: A worked example of a co-branded playbook: pair change-risk review with a full bug hunt, then turn the findings into verified commits. This is the template a partner tool or creator drops their brand into — the content and cut are shared.

partner.name: Partner Tool
partner.blurb: This slot shows how a co-branded playbook appears — a partner's logo, name, and voice wrap a curated sequence, and the revenue is split. Swap in a real collaborator to ship one.

tagline: Example co-branded review sequence — how partner playbooks look.

name: Harden Before Ship
desc: The security pass before you expose anything: secrets, access control, injection paths, and an attacker's-eye threat model — then the Fixer turns the findings into verified commits.
tagline: Lock the doors before you ship — then ship the fixes as commits.

name: Data-Layer Tune-Up
desc: The runtime data layer, end to end: the queries that will not scale, the integrity the schema does not enforce, and whether you could actually recover from data loss.
tagline: Make the data layer fast, sound, and recoverable.

name: DX Tune-Up
desc: Sharpen the developer inner loop: time the edit-to-feedback cycle, make failures easy to diagnose, and shorten the wait between a push and a trustworthy CI signal.
tagline: Cut the friction out of every change your team makes.

name: Test Confidence
desc: Earn a green suite you can trust: find the coverage gaps, strengthen the tests that assert nothing, kill the flakes, and rebalance a top-heavy pyramid.
tagline: Make green actually mean green.

name: Ship a Public API
desc: Everything a developer meets when they build on you: webhooks, the SDK, versioning, rate limits, and the first-hour docs — then the Fixer ships the fixes as commits.
tagline: Make an API developers actually want to build on.

name: Responsible AI Review
desc: The responsibility pass for an AI product: fairness across users, grounded and honest outputs, prompt-injection resistance, transparency, and the provenance of the data behind the model.
tagline: Is your AI fair, grounded, honest, and defensible?

name: Production Resilience
desc: Whether it survives a bad day: graceful degradation when a dependency dies, the failure experiments worth running, the first bottleneck under load, abuse defenses, and incident readiness.
tagline: Rehearse the bad day before it happens.

name: Privacy & Compliance
desc: The operational-privacy layer regulators actually check: honest consent, real data-subject rights, encryption and key management, and a trustworthy audit trail.
tagline: Respect the user — and be able to prove it.

name: Make It Fast
desc: A speed-only sweep: profile the hot paths, hold a latency budget, fix the queries that will not scale, cut the bundle the browser downloads, and mine the churny hotspots.
tagline: Five audits, one goal — make it fast.

name: Inherit a Codebase
desc: Taking over an unfamiliar or orphaned project: get onboarded, read its health, map the debt, and find where the knowledge is dangerously concentrated.
tagline: Everything to learn before you own someone else's code.

name: Refactor Safely
desc: Change with a net: assess the risk of the change, shore up the tests around it, simplify, and let the Fixer land the cleanup as verified commits.
tagline: Refactor without holding your breath.

name: Cost Down
desc: Take money off the bill: audit infrastructure spend, the token economics of any AI, dead weight to prune, and the latency budget that right-sizes resources.
tagline: Find the spend you can cut without anyone noticing.

name: Agent Cost Control
desc: For AI-agent teams: the token economics of the loop, whether the model tier fits the job, and the context engineering that stops you paying for tokens you do not need.
tagline: Stop overpaying for every agent call.

name: New-Year Reset
desc: A themed start-of-year sweep: read the vitals, prune the dead weight, simplify what remains, and synthesize next year's roadmap — one seasonal bundle.
tagline: Start the year on a clean, clear codebase.

name: Sponsored Speed Bundle
desc: A worked example of a sponsored playbook: a curated performance sequence a sponsor's brand wraps — logo, name, and voice around shared content, with the placement paid. Swap in a real sponsor to ship one.

partner.name: Sponsor Name
partner.blurb: This slot shows how a sponsored bundle appears — a sponsor's logo, name, and blurb wrap a curated sequence, and the placement is paid. Swap in a real sponsor to ship one.

tagline: Example sponsored bundle — how a paid placement looks.
